Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

This patch breaks local testing with:

Formatting mgs, mds, osts
Format mds1: /tmp/lustre-mdt1
mkfs.lustre: Failed to get realpath to /tmp/lustre-mdt1: No such file or directory
mkfs.lustre: exiting with 2 (No such file or directory)

Patch Set 3:

Oleg, this is not a bug. The new code does not create loopback files automatically. I ever thought this was a problem and reported it to LU-3991. Please see the comments from Andreas and Alex on http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/7722/ for patch set #1 and #2.

Andreas said that "I don't think that creating loopback files automatically is a good idea. In the normal use case (instead if during testing) any typo or error in the device name will result in a loopback file being created instead if returning a useful error to the user. For example, if someone accidentally runs "mkfs.lustre /dev/sfa" instead of "sda" this will result in a loopback file being created in /dev that will initially work, but will be very slow and will later fail when the root Filesystem runs pit of space.

I would rather that testers/scripts make sure that the loopback file it created first, and allow mkfs.lustre to return an error if this us not done correctly."

Patch Set 3:

Emoly, I think it is fine if test-framework.sh creates loopback files during testing, but not if it is done by mkfs.lustre and can cause problems for users.  It looks like mkfs_opts() might be a central place for it?

Patch Set 3:

Oleg, I agree with Andreas to create files in test-framework.sh. So, could you land this patch?

Patch Set 3:

as we discussed on skype - this patch needs to be recreated to do the file creation from test framework so that the local tetsing is not broken.

Patch Set 3:

Yes, I'm working on this and will refresh the patch later.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1

(4 comments)

I think this patch is OK to fix the problem, but I agree with John's comment:

  A more robust approach than using real paths would be
  to stat() the device and each of the entries from the
  first column of /proc/mounts and compare st_rdev members.

That would reduce the need to understand all of the details of loop devices.

Patch Set 6:

John, if the given file is already associated wit a mounted loop device, stat() method as you suggested can't help in my following test:

[root@centos6-2 tests]# ls -al /tmp/mdt1
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 16 Feb  7 10:52 /tmp/mdt1 -> /tmp/lustre-mdt1

[root@centos6-2 tests]# mount
...
/dev/loop0 on /mnt/mds1 type lustre (rw,loop=/dev/loop0)
/dev/loop1 on /mnt/ost1 type lustre (rw,loop=/dev/loop1)
/dev/loop2 on /mnt/ost2 type lustre (rw,loop=/dev/loop2)
centos6-2@tcp:/lustre on /mnt/lustre type lustre (rw,user_xattr,flock)

[root@centos6-2 tests]# losetup -a
/dev/loop0: [fd00]:1570 (/tmp/lustre-mdt1)
/dev/loop1: [fd00]:43290 (/tmp/lustre-ost1)
/dev/loop2: [fd00]:43291 (/tmp/lustre-ost2)

[root@centos6-2 tests]# ./test_stat 
filename: /dev/loop0, st_ino: 5591, st_dev: 5, st_rdev: 1792
filename: /tmp/lustre-mdt1, st_ino: 1570, st_dev: 64768, st_rdev: 0
filename: /tmp/mdt1, st_ino: 1570, st_dev: 64768, st_rdev: 0

In this example, comparing st_rdev member of /dev/loop0 and /tmp/mdt1 doesn't make sense. Please correct me if I miss anything.

Patch Set 6:

Looks good to me.

parents.subject: LU-5315 mkfs: use the relative path to locate modules

commit.subject: LU-3682 tunefs: prevent tunefs running on a mounted device
commit.message:
LU-3682 tunefs: prevent tunefs running on a mounted device

This patch is to check if the given device is already mounted in the
following situations:
1) For ldiskfs, get the real path to the given device in case it is a
   symlink or dm device, and return EEXIST if it is already mounted.
   What's more, if the given path doesn't exist, mkfs.lustre won't
   create it automatically. So to avoid breaking the local testing,
   this patch creates the file in test-framework.sh instead.
2) For zfs, just pass the given pool name directly.
3) If the given file is already associated with a mounted loop device,
   check_mtab_entry() should return EEXIST to prevent the next loop
   setup attemp.
Also, this patch adds conf-sanity.sh test_81 to verify the fix.

Signed-off-by: Emoly Liu <emoly.liu@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ib45a710d193dc0806878a676cc36bdad9fb7fea6
